free_text: Fission yields in the thermal neutron fission of 233U,
235U, 239Pu and 241Pu
Preliminary data in table
for fission yields of long lived and stable fission
products in thermal neutron fission of 241Pu.
preliminary data in table
neutron flux of 10**12 n/cm2/s.
Purified solution of 233-U (99.20 atom% 233-U),
and 241-Pu (78.37 atom% 241-Pu and 5.20 atom% 239-Pu)
were used in the experiment.
For gamma spectrometric method 10 microgm target
nuclide was electrodeposited on platinum as backing
material. Aluminum catcher foils of 0.025 mm
thickness were placed on the target.
For radiochemical method 30 microgm target nuclide was
evaporated on 0.025 mm thick superpure aluminum foil.
For analyses using mass spectrometry, 0.5 to 1 mg of
target nuclide was evaporated in thin walled quartz
ampoule. Each sealed ampoule was wrapped in superpure
aluminum foil.
For the gamma spectrometric measurements, the
catcher foils were mounted and counted in a fixed
geometry of Ge(Li)detector coupled to a multichannel
analyser.
The radiochemical measurement was followed on
an end window gas flow beta proportional counter.
Mass spectrometric isotope measurements were
done using CH-5 and MAT-261 thermal ionization
mass spectrometer.
The targets for mass spectrometric method were
irradiated for 15, 30, and 90 days.
For the gamma spectrometric and the radiochemical
methods were irradiated for a period of 10 t0 24 hrs.
For the gamma spectrometric measurements the
catcher foils were mounted and counted in a fixed
geometry on a Ge(Li) detector coupled to a multichannel
analyser. The system had a resolution of 2.2 keV
at 660 keV. The fission product activity was followed
for its decay. The photopeak area was obtained by
subtraction of linear component from the sum of channel
contents defining the peak.
The radiochemical separations of the fission
115-Cd, 121-Sn, 123-Sn, 125-Sn, 127-Sb and 140-Ba were
carried out using standard radiochemical procedures and
measurement of their activities were carried out using
end window gas flow beta proportional counter.
For mass spectrometric determinations the
sample in quartz ampoule was dissolved after a cooling
period of 2 months to 1 year. Isotopic ratios were
determined using CH-5 and MAT-261 thermal ionization
mass spectrometers after chemical treatment.
Preferential evaporation and ionization technique was
used for sequential determination of isotopic ratios of
Rb, Cs, Ba, Sr, Ce, Eu, Nd and Zr from the same
filament loading. Mo and Ru isotope ratios were
determined after purification from Zr as well as other
fission products.
Thermal neutron spectra
The estimated uncertainties on the fission
yield values determined using the mass spectrometric
method are   1-3%.
The estimated uncertainties on the fission yield values
in asymmetric region are 4% and about 12% for symmetric
region for gamma spectrometric region and the
radiochemical methods.
